name: Chinese Risotto with Shiitake Mushrooms
recipeIngredient: 2 teaspoons Minced red jalapeno or serrano chili
5 ounces Fresh shiitake mushrooms, stemmed and sliced
1 cup Chopped boneless smoked pork chops
3 cups (or more) chicken stock or canned low-salt broth
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 1 tablespoon sesame oil in a heavy medium skillet over medium-high heat.
2. Add minced chili and saute for 1 minute.
3. Add garlic and saute for 1 minute, then remove from heat.
4. In the same skillet, add soy sauce, rice vinegar, and pinch of sugar. Stir to blend.
5. Strain through a fine sieve into a small bowl, pressing on solids with the back of a spoon. (Soy sauce mixture can be prepared up to 1 day ahead. Refrigerate.)
6. Heat 3 tablespoons vegetable oil in a heavy large saucepan over medium heat.
7. Add chopped shallots and saute for 2 minutes.
8. Add sliced shiitake mushrooms and smoked pork, and saute for 6 minutes.
9. Add rice and stir for 1 minute.
10. Add 3 cups chicken stock and bring to a simmer, stirring occasionally. Reduce heat, cover, and simmer until the rice is just tender but still firm to bite, stirring occasionally, about 20 minutes.
11. Add chopped mustard greens and cilantro, and stir for 1 minute.
12. Mix in the soy sauce mixture. If the risotto is dry, add more chicken stock by tablespoons.
13. Divide the risotto among bowls and serve.
